blocks:
Ayahs

قُلْ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ أَنَّهُ ٱسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا قُرْءَانًا عَجَبًا (۱)
Say, ‘It has been revealed to me that a team of the jinn listened [to the Quran] and they said, “Indeed we heard a wonderful Quran, (1)
يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلرُّشْدِ فَـَٔامَنَّا بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَن نُّشْرِكَ بِرَبِّنَآ أَحَدًا (۲)
which guides to rectitude. Hence, we have believed in it and we will never ascribe any partner to our Lord. (2)

Key Points

Nafar (نَفر) refers to a group of three to seven or ten people. In the divine worldview, existence is not limited to what is visible, nor is the visible restricted to what we observe today. A human being who, every so often, discovers a galaxy containing billions of stars, whose distances are measured in light-years, should not expect to be completely familiar with all of existence. Therefore, we submit ourselves to revelation (Wahy) and believe in what the Quran has stated. The Quran contains many verses about the Jinn, some of which we will mention:

  1. The creation of the Jinn was before that of humans. «والجانّ خلقناه من قبل» (19)
  2. The Jinn hold a status similar to that of humans, because the purpose of their creation is like the purpose of human creation. «و ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس الاّ لیعبدون» (20) I did not create the Jinn and the humans except that they should worship Me.
  3. They are subject to command and prohibition, address and reproach. «یا معشر الجنّ و الانس» (21)
  4. They possess the power of choice, and thus a group of them are believers (Mu'min) and a group are disbelievers (Kafir).
  5. They were created from fire, and Satan is of that species. «كان من الجنّ» (22)
  6. Although we do not see him, he sees us. «انّه یراكم هو و قبیله من حیث لاترونهم» (23)
  7. They can serve as helpers for the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah). The Quran states: they worked for Prophet Sulayman (Solomon). «یعملون له» (24)

All astonishing and innovative matters become ordinary after a few days, but the Quran is an amazing Book that is eternal and never becomes ordinary. Imam Ali عليه السلام says in Nahj al-Balaghah: «لاتفنى عجائبه» (Its wonders never cease) (25). All astonishing matters are astonishing from one perspective, but the Quran is so from countless aspects, as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: «لا تحصى عجائبه» (Its wonders cannot be counted) (26). The Quran is unique in terms of its wording, content, comprehensiveness, and harmony with human nature (Fitrah) and needs.

We read in a Hadith: Whoever keeps company with the Quran gains guidance and perfect growth in one respect, and his ignorance and misguidance are removed in another respect. «ما جالس احد هذا القرآن الاّ قام عنه بزیادة او نقصان؛ زیادة فى هُدى او نقصان من عمى» (No one sits with this Quran except that he rises from it with an increase or a decrease; an increase in guidance or a decrease in blindness) (27).

We observe several types of reactions to the Quran: 1. Hearing and believing: «سمعنا قرآناً عجباً... فآمنّا» 2. Hearing and increase in faith: «اذا تلیت علیهم ایاته زادتهم ایماناً» (28) 3. Hearing and evading: «اذا انزلت سورة... استأذنك اولوا الطول منهم» (29), «فاذا انزلت سورة محكمة وذكر فیها القتال رأیت الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض ینظرون الیك» (30) 4. Selective acceptance: «افتؤمنون ببعض الكتاب و تكفرون ببعض» (31) 5. Hearing and slandering: «ان هذا الّا اساطیر الاولین» (32), «بل قالوا اضغاث احلام» (33) 6. Hearing and belittling: «لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» (34) 7. Campaigning against hearing it: «لاتسمعوا لهذا القرآن و الغوا فیه» (35)

The Jinn understood the merits of the Quran and said: «قرآناً عجباً» (an amazing Quran), but the stubborn Arabs said: The Quran is not amazing, and if we wished, we could produce something similar. «لونشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» (36).

The path of growth (Rushd) is the same as the path of truth (Haqq). «یهدى الى الرشد» (guides to growth), «یهدى الى الحق» (guides to the truth) (37). It is interesting that both these expressions come from the Jinn.

(19) Hijr, 27. (20) Dhariyat, 56. (21) Ar-Rahman, 23. (22) Kahf, 50. (23) A'raf, 27. (24) Saba', 13. (25) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 75. (26) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 599. (27) Ghurar al-Hikam. (28) Anfal, 2. (29) Tawbah, 86. (30) Muhammad, 20. (31) Baqarah, 85. (32) An'am, 25. (33) Anbiya, 5. (34) Anfal, 31. (35) Fussilat, 26. (36) Anfal, 31. (37) Ahqaf, 30.

Messages

1- The Prophet did not personally see the Jinn but became aware of their listening through revelation (Wahy). «اوحى الىّ انّه استمع»

2- Although the Jinn are not made of earth, they listen to human speech. «استمع نفر من الجن»

3- Listening to the recitation of the Quran has an impact on one's being. «استمع... فقالوا... قرآناً عجبا»

4- The Quran differs from all other books in terms of its wording and content. «قرآناً عجبا»

5- We should listen to speech that leads us to growth (Rushd). «سمعنا قرآناً... یهدى الى الرشد»

6- The Jinn possess the power of hearing («سمعنا»), speaking («فقالوا»), and analysis («یهدى الى الرشد»).

7- The guidance of the Quran is continuous. «یهدى الى الرشد»

8- True human growth (Rushd) is spiritual growth. «یهدى الى الرّشد»

9- Possessing the means of growth is not enough; achieving growth is what matters. «قرآناً عجباً یهدى الى الرّشد فآمنّابه»

10- The sign of growth is faith and sincerity. «یهدى الى الرشد فآمنّا به ولن نشرك»

11- If readiness exists, upon hearing a few verses of the Quran, a person will understand the truth, invite others to it, and take a firm stance against deviations. «سمعنا... یهدى الى الرشد فآمنّا به ولم نشرك بربّنا احداً»

12- Faith is complete only when it is never accompanied by polytheism (Shirk). «آمنّا به ولم نشرك بربّنا احدا»

Ayahs

وَأَنَّهُۥ تَعَـٰلَىٰ جَدُّ رَبِّنَا مَا ٱتَّخَذَ صَـٰحِبَةً وَلَا وَلَدًا (۳)
Exalted be the majesty of our Lord; He has taken neither any spouse nor son. (3)
وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ يَقُولُ سَفِيهُنَا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ شَطَطًا (٤)
The foolish ones among us used to speak atrocious lies concerning Allah. (4)
وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن تَقُولَ ٱلْإِنسُ وَٱلْجِنُّ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا (٥)
We thought that humans and jinn would never utter any falsehood concerning Allah. (5)

Key Points

The word "Jadd" (جَدّ) means greatness/majesty. If it is used for a grandfather, it stems from the greatness and majesty attributed to him within the family. "Shaṭaṭ" (شطط) means oppressive speech and speech far from the truth. The intent of "Safīhunā" (سفیهنا) is either the group of the senseless or the person of Iblīs, who was from the Jinn, objected to Allah, and strayed from the truth.

Messages

1- The first step toward growth (or guidance) is knowing Allah. «یهدى الى الرشد - انّه تعالى جدّ ربّنا...»

2- The Jinn also know that Allah has neither a wife nor a son. «ما اتّخذ صاحبة و لا ولداً»

3- Having a wife and son is far removed from the status of Allah's Oneness and Self-Sufficiency. «تعالى جدّ ربّنا ما اتّخذ صاحبة و لا ولداً»

4- Speaking falsehood is a sign of foolishness (safāhah). «یقول سفیهنا على اللّه شططا»

5- The Jinn are aware of the thoughts and beliefs of humans. «و انّا ظنّنا ان لن تقول الانس و الجن على اللّه كذباً»

Ayahs

وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ رِجَالٌ مِّنَ ٱلْإِنسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجَالٍ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَزَادُوهُمْ رَهَقًا (٦)
Indeed some persons from the humans would seek the protection of some persons from the jinn, thus only adding to their rebellion. (6)
وَأَنَّهُمْ ظَنُّوا۟ كَمَا ظَنَنتُمْ أَن لَّن يَبْعَثَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدًا (۷)
They thought, just as you think, that Allah will not raise anyone from the dead. (7)
وَأَنَّا لَمَسْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ فَوَجَدْنَـٰهَا مُلِئَتْ حَرَسًا شَدِيدًا وَشُهُبًا (۸)
Indeed we made for the heaven and found it full of mighty sentries and flames. (8)

Key Points

"Rahq" (رَهَق) means an ugly and wrongful deed that encompasses a person. The intent behind seeking refuge with the jinn, according to narrations, is going to those who have contact with the jinn and are inspired by Satan, and asking them to solve one's problem.(38) Whoever is enlightened by the light of the Quran and achieves growth is saved from all intellectual and practical deviations. After the phrase "yuhdī ilā ar-rushd" (guides to the right path), the Quran describes the characteristics of deviant beliefs: those who attribute a spouse and child to Allah ("mā attakhadha ṣāḥibatan wa lā waladan"), those who speak falsehood ("yaqūlu safīhunā ‘alā Allāhi shaṭaṭan"), those who seek refuge with the jinn instead of Allah ("ya‘ūdhūna bi-rijālin mina al-jinn"), and those who imagine there is no Resurrection ("lan yab‘atha Allāhu aḥadan").

38) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Seeking refuge with the jinn is a vain and foolish act. "yaqūlū ‘alā Allāhi safīhunā ‘alā Allāhi shaṭaṭan... ya‘ūdhūna bi-rijālin mina al-jinn"

2- The jinn, like humans, have males and females. "bi-rijālin mina al-jinn"

3- The consequence of contact with the jinn, which is generally for achieving improper goals, increases the deviation of both parties. "ya‘ūdhūna... fa-zādūhum rahaqan"

4- Deniers of the Resurrection have no scientific proof or evidence; rather, they are afflicted by conjecture and assumption. "ẓannū kamā ẓanantum"

5- The jinn are aware of human beliefs. "ẓannū kamā ẓanantum"

6- Some of the jinn are disbelievers and deny the Resurrection. "lan yab‘atha Allāhu aḥadan"

7- The jinn have the ability to make contact with the heaven. "lamasnā as-samā’"

8- Do not allow the unworthy access everywhere. "fa-wajadnāhu muli’at ḥarasan shadīdan"

Ayahs

وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقْعُدُ مِنْهَا مَقَـٰعِدَ لِلسَّمْعِ ۖ فَمَن يَسْتَمِعِ ٱلْـَٔانَ يَجِدْ لَهُۥ شِهَابًا رَّصَدًا (۹)
We used to sit in its positions to eavesdrop, but anyone listening now finds a flame waiting for him. (9)
وَأَنَّا لَا نَدْرِىٓ أَشَرٌّ أُرِيدَ بِمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ أَرَادَ بِهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ رَشَدًا (۱٠)
We do not know whether ill is intended for those who are on the earth, or whether their Lord intends good for them. (10)
وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلصَّـٰلِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ كُنَّا طَرَآئِقَ قِدَدًا (۱۱)
Among us some are righteous and some are otherwise: we are various sects. (11)
وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن نُّعْجِزَ ٱللَّهَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَن نُّعْجِزَهُۥ هَرَبًا (۱۲)
We know that we cannot frustrate Allah on the earth, nor can we frustrate Him by fleeing. (12)

Key Points

Rasad (رَصَد) is the plural noun (ism jam') of rasid (راصد), meaning observer or guard. Qidad (قِدَد) is the plural of qadd (قدّ), meaning cut off, and tarā'iq qidadan (طرائق قددا) means various paths or ways. It is inferred from these verses that the believers among the Jinn, besides recognizing the Quran, have realized their own weakness, the greatness of Divine power, and their own power of choice and free will.

Messages

1- The revelation of the Quran also had an effect on the heavens. Before the revelation of the Quran, the Jinn used to eavesdrop on celestial news, but they were prevented from doing so afterward. «انّا كنّا نقعد منها... فمن یستمع الآن»

2- The Jinn do not know the future of humanity or the world. «و انّا لا ندرى...»

3- The punishment for eavesdropping is a severe strike. «شهاباً رصداً»

4- In the Quran, goodness and virtue are attributed to Allah, but evil and badness are not attributed to Him; they are mentioned in the passive voice. «اشرّ اُرید... اراد بهم ربّهم رشدّا»

5- Granting guidance (or rectitude) is among the functions of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّهم رشداً»

6- The nature and disposition (fitrah) of the Jinn are not based on corruption and mischief; there are righteous and unrighteous individuals among them. «منّا الصّالحون و منّا دون ذلك»

7- The necessity of free will and freedom is the formation of groups. «كنّا طرائق قدداً»

8- Neither by resistance nor by flight can one frustrate Allah. «لن نعجز اللّه فى الارض و لن نعجزه هربا»

Ayahs

وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعْنَا ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ ۖ فَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِرَبِّهِۦ فَلَا يَخَافُ بَخْسًا وَلَا رَهَقًا (۱۳)
When we heard the [message of] guidance, we believed in it. Whoever that has faith in his Lord will fear neither privation nor oppression. (13)
وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلْمُسْلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا ٱلْقَـٰسِطُونَ ۖ فَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ تَحَرَّوْا۟ رَشَدًا (۱٤)
Among us some are Muslims and some of us are perverse.” ’ Those who submit [to Allah]—it is they who pursue rectitude. (14)
وَأَمَّا ٱلْقَـٰسِطُونَ فَكَانُوا۟ لِجَهَنَّمَ حَطَبًا (۱٥)
As for the perverse, they will be firewood for hell. (15)

Key Points

Bakhs (بخس) means unjust reduction (or diminution). Rahaq (رَهَق) is used in the sense of foolishness, transgression, sin, falsehood, and oppression. (39) Qist (قِسط) means share and portion. A Qasit (قاسط) is someone who takes the share of another, and a Muqsit (مقسط) is someone who gives others their share. Technically, if it is derived from the if'āl form (باب افعال), it means establishing justice, and if it is the simple triliteral form (ثلاثى مجرد), it means oppressing others. The word taharrau (تحرّوا) means to intend or seek. Since Qasitūn (قاسطون - the unjust) is used in contrast to Muslimūn (مسلمون - the submitters), the people of submission must be committed to justice. Since the verses of this Surah up to this point have been the statements of the Jinn who heard the Quran and then expressed their thoughts, beliefs, and views to other Jinn, paying attention to their style of speech and the content of their statements can be useful for preachers in the discussion of religious propagation (tablīgh). Now we briefly review some of them:

  • Listening attentively. «استمع»
  • Group presence. «نفر من الجنّ»
  • Rejoicing and showing emotion in the face of truth. «سمعنا قرآناً عجبا»
  • Propagation/Preaching. «فقالوا... یهدى الى الرشد»
  • Expressing belief and inclination. «فامنّا به »
  • Declaring dissociation from deviations. «ولن نشرك بربّنا احداً»
  • Rejecting false beliefs. «ما اتّخذ صاحبة و لا ولدا»
  • Belittling the unworthy. «یقول سفیهنا على اللّه شططا»
  • Regretting the deviation of others. «ظنّنا ان لن تقول الانس والجن على اللّه كذبا»
  • Negating superstitions and false inclinations. «رجال من الانس یعوذون برجال من الجنّ»
  • Stating the greatness of the Truth (Allah). «فمن یستمع الان یجد له شهاباً رصداً»
  • Stating the power of the Truth (Allah). «لن نعجز اللّه»
  • Stating the weakness of man. «لن نعجزه هربا»
  • Giving hope to the people of faith. «فمن یؤمن بربّه فلا یخاف بخسا و لا رهقا»
  • Comparing Muslims and oppressors. «منّا المسلمون و منّا القاسطون»
  • Calling others through our actions. «سمعنا الهدى آمنّا به فمن یؤمن بربّه ...»
  • Highlighting the good people and good deeds. «منّا الصالحون و منّا دون ذلك»
  • Starting with pivotal and fundamental issues. «یؤمن بربّه، سمعنا قرآناً، لن نعجزه، لجهنّم حطبا»

39) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- The Quran is guidance entirely. «سمعنا قرآناً...سمعنا الهدى»

2- Speed in good deeds increases their value. «لمّا سمعنا...آمنّا به»

3- Faith in the Quran is faith in the Lord of the worlds. «سمعنا... آمنا به...یؤمن بربّه»

4- Faith guarantees a reward without reduction or deficiency. «فلا یخاف بخساً و لا رهقاً»

5- A Muslim seeks justice. «منّا المسلمون و منّا القاسطون»

6- Although the Jinn are subtle beings, their punishment is physical. «لجهنّم حطبا»

7- Both the sinful human is the fuel of the Fire: «وقودها النّاس والحجارة» (40) and the deviant Jinn are the firewood of the Fire. «فكانوا الجهنم حطباً» 40) Al-Baqarah, 24.

Ayahs

وَأَلَّوِ ٱسْتَقَـٰمُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْنَـٰهُم مَّآءً غَدَقًا (۱٦)
If they are steadfast on the path [of Allah], We shall provide them with abundant water, (16)
لِّنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن يُعْرِضْ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِۦ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًا صَعَدًا (۱۷)
so that We may test them therein, and whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will let him into an escalating punishment. (17)

Key Points

«Ghadaq» means abundance, and «Sa‘ad» means difficult and hard. Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: The meaning of «istāqāmū ‘alā al-ṭarīqah» (they remained steadfast on the path) is steadfastness on the path of the Ahl al-Bayt. And it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that if people remain steadfast upon our Wilayah (guardianship/authority), they will benefit from abundant knowledge. (41) «La-asqaynāhum mā’an ghadaqā» (We would certainly give them abundant water to drink) — one of the best educational methods is the method of comparison. In one Ayah, He mentions steadfastness and rain, and in the next Ayah, turning away and punishment. By comparing these two states, the issue of truth and falsehood becomes clearer. Turning away from the remembrance of Allah makes life difficult in this world as well. «Wa man a‘raḍa ‘an dhikrī fa-inna lahu ma‘īshatan ḍankā» (And whoever turns away from My remembrance—indeed, for him is a restricted life) (42) And in the Hereafter, it afflicts man with a severe and comprehensive punishment. «Wa man yu‘riḍ ‘an dhikri Rabbihī yaslukhu ‘adhāban ṣa‘adā»

(41) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. (42) Taha, 124.

Messages

1- More important than faith is perseverance and steadfastness upon it. «Wa an law istaqāmū ‘alā al-ṭarīqah»

2- Among the paths, only one path of truth is acceptable. «ṭarā’iqa qidadā... ‘alā al-ṭarīqah»

3- Stating the effects and blessings of faith is the best method of invitation. «law istaqāmū... la-asqaynāhum»

4- The effects of faith are not only in the Hereafter but also manifest in this world. «La-asqaynāhum mā’an ghadaqā»

5- Steadfastness on the path of truth is valuable. Not every steadfastness is valuable, as some forms of steadfastness are obstinacy and stubbornness. «Istaqāmū ‘alā al-ṭarīqah»

6- Expansion of sustenance is one of the means of trial. «La-asqaynāhum mā’an ghadaqā linftanahum fīh» (Just as Prophet Sulayman also considered the expansion of his own sustenance a means of trial. «Hādhā min faḍli Rabbī li-yabluwanī» (43))

7- Steadfastness on the straight path affects nature. «Mā’an ghadaqā»

8- Prosperity is a ground for heedlessness. «Mā’an ghadaqā linftanahum fīh»

9- Do not become arrogant due to the expansion of sustenance. «Linftanahum fīh»

10- Even after steadfastness on the path of truth and receiving the reward, do not consider your work finished, for a new trial is just beginning. «Mā’an ghadaqā linftanahum fīh...»

11- Allah possesses both mercy and wrath. «Mā’an ghadaqā... ‘adhāban ṣa‘adā»

12- One of the factors leading to Hell is turning away from the remembrance of Allah. «Wa man yu‘riḍ ‘an dhikri Rabbihī yaslukhu ‘adhāban ṣa‘adā»

13- Sometimes the descent of rain is a means of trial, which, if accompanied by ingratitude and disbelief, leads to comprehensive wrath. «‘Adhāban ṣa‘adā»

(43) An-Naml, 40.

Ayahs

وَأَنَّ ٱلْمَسَـٰجِدَ لِلَّهِ فَلَا تَدْعُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدًا (۱۸)
The places of worship belong to Allah, so do not invoke anyone along with Allah. (18)
وَأَنَّهُۥ لَمَّا قَامَ عَبْدُ ٱللَّهِ يَدْعُوهُ كَادُوا۟ يَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِ لِبَدًا (۱۹)
When the servant of Allah rose to pray to Him, they almost crowded around him. (19)
قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَدْعُوا۟ رَبِّى وَلَآ أُشْرِكُ بِهِۦٓ أَحَدًا (۲٠)
Say, ‘I pray only to my Lord, and I do not ascribe any partner to Him.’ (20)
قُلْ إِنِّى لَآ أَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا رَشَدًا (۲۱)
Say, ‘I have no power to bring you any harm or good [of my own accord].’ (21)

Key Points

Masājid (مساجد) is the plural of masjid (مسجد), meaning the place of prostration (sajdah). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Because the Jews and Christians committed polytheism (shirk) in their places of worship, Allah said: Do not call upon anyone alongside Allah in the Islamic mosques, and may Allah curse anyone who does not respect the mosque. (44) Lubadan (لُبدا) means the accumulation of a crowd and congestion. In the phrase «فلا تدعوا مع اللّه احدا» (Do not call upon anyone alongside Allah), du'ā (calling/supplication) means worship, based on the Ayah: «ادعونى استجب لكم انّ الّذین یستكبرون عن عبادتى» (Call upon Me, I will respond to you. Indeed, those who disdain My worship...) (45) Therefore, calling upon anyone other than Allah as an act of worship is forbidden (harām), but ordinary calling is not polytheism (shirk). The intent behind the crowding of people when the Prophet was worshipping, as the Ayah states: «لمّا قام عبد اللّه یدعوه كادوا یكونون علیه لبدا» (When the servant of Allah stood up calling upon Him, they almost crowded upon him in masses), is either the crowding of opponents for mockery and harassment, or the crowding of lovers from among the jinn and humans for learning.

Sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) reaches its peak when a person does not see himself, but sees everything as coming from Him (Allah). In these Ayahs, He first says: I do not associate anyone with Him. Then He says: I do not even consider myself the owner of anything. «لا اشركُ به احداً... لا املك لكم ضراً و لا رشدا» (I do not associate anyone with Him... I possess for you neither harm nor right direction.) The manifestation of the Prophet's servitude is that He should not claim any role for Himself, but know that everything belongs to Him (Allah) and say: «لا املك لكم ضراً ولا رشدا» (I possess for you neither harm nor right direction.)

From the beginning of the Sūrah until here, the word rushd (right direction/guidance) has been used four times so that the objective of the revelation is constantly kept in mind. «یهدى الى الرشد» (guides to the right direction), «اراد بهم ربّهم رشداً» (their Lord intended for them right direction), «تحرّوا رشداً» (sought the right direction), «ضراً ولا رشداً» (harm nor right direction).

The intent behind the Prophet not possessing benefit or harm is that He does not possess them independently of Allah; otherwise, in numerous Ayahs, an important role is established for the Prophet by Allah. Such as seeking forgiveness for the people: «واستغفر لهم الرّسول لوجدوا اللّه توّابا» (and the Messenger sought forgiveness for them, they would have found Allah Forgiving) (46), «تعالوا یستغفر لكم رسول اللّه» (Come, so the Messenger of Allah may ask forgiveness for you) (47).

Several points regarding the Mosque

  1. The first structure on Earth was a mosque. «انّ اول بیت وضع للناس للذى ببكّة مباركا» (Indeed, the first House established for mankind was that at Bakkah, blessed) (48)
  2. The location of the mosque must possess sanctity. Just as the believers said: We will surely take over them a mosque (at the site of the Companions of the Cave). «نتّخذن علیهم مسجداً» (49)
  3. The Prophet's first action in Medina was building a mosque. «لمسجد اسّس على التقوى من اوّل یوم» (a mosque founded upon piety from the first day) (50)
  4. The name Al-Masjid Al-Harām (the Sacred Mosque) is mentioned more than ten times in the Quran.
  5. Allah has considered the mosque His House and appointed the best of people—namely, Abraham and Ishmael, two great Prophets—as its servants. «طهّرا بیتى» (Purify My House) (51)
  6. Adornment is recommended for attending the mosque. «خذوا زینتكم عند كلّ مسجد» (Take your adornment at every place of prostration) (52)
  7. If the construction of a mosque is not based on piety, and the mosque becomes a factor for division, it must be destroyed. «مسجداً ضرارا» (a mosque intended to cause harm) (53)
  8. The sanctity of the mosque is such that not everyone has the right to build or maintain it. «ما كان للمشركین ان یعمروا مساجد اللّه» (It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of Allah) (54). This matter is exclusive to the pious believers. «انما یعمر مساجد اللّه من آمن باللّه... ولم یخش الاّ اللّه» (Only those should maintain the mosques of Allah who believe in Allah... and fear none but Allah) (55)
  9. The mosque and other centers of worship for Jews and Christians (monasteries, hermitages, and temples) are so valuable that blood must be shed to protect them. «...لهدّمت صوامع و بیع و صلوات» (...monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques would have been destroyed) (56)
  10. The mosque has etiquette, including that conversations should not be held loudly within it, vain talk should not be raised, buying and selling should not be discussed, and frivolous matters should be avoided.
  11. There is a reward for every step taken toward the mosque.
  12. Sitting in the mosque awaiting prayer has a reward.
  13. A mosque in which prayer is not performed complains to Allah.
  14. The neighbor of a mosque has no worth if he does not perform his prayer in the mosque.
  15. Allah removes punishment from others because of the people of the mosque. (57)
  16. A person was returning sadly from Mu'taṣim (the Abbasid Caliph) and recounted: A thief confessed to his theft, and the Caliph gathered the jurists (fuqahā) and asked them for the ruling. One said: The thief's hand must be cut off from the wrist, because in the Ayah of Tayammum (dry ablution) we read: «فامسحوا بوجوهكم و ایدیكم» (Wipe your faces and your hands) (58). Another said: Cut the hand from the elbow, because the Quran says in the Ayah of Wudu (ablution): «و ایدیكم الى المرافق» (and your hands up to the elbows). Then Mu'taṣim, who had previously invited Imam Jawad عليه السلام, asked His Holiness for the ruling on theft. Imam Jawad عليه السلام said: Only four fingers should be cut off. Because the Quran says: «وانّ المساجد للّه» (And the mosques are for Allah), the place of prostration is exclusive to Allah, and whatever rests on the ground during prostration should not be severed. Mu'taṣim approved of the Imam's argument عليه السلام, and His Holiness's view was acted upon. (59)

44) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 72, p. 355. 45) Mu’min, 60. 46) Munafiqūn, 5. 47) Munafiqūn, 5. 48) Āl ‘Imrān, 96. 49) Kahf, 21. 50) Tawbah, 108. 51) Baqarah, 125. 52) A‘rāf, 31. 53) Tawbah, 107. 54) Tawbah, 17. 55) Tawbah, 18. 56) Hajj, 41. 57) Items 10 to 15 are based on narrations found in Mizan al-Hikmah, chapter on Mosque. 58) Nisā’, 43. 59) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah, Kitāb al-Hudūd, Hadd al-Sariqah.

Messages

  1. Places of worship belong to Allah. «انّ المساجد للّه»
  2. The seven points that are placed on the ground during prostration are exclusive to Allah, and prostration to anyone other than Allah is forbidden. «وانّ المساجد للّه»
  3. A place built with the intention of being a mosque, and where prayer is performed, acquires special rulings. «وانّ المساجد للّه فلا تدعوا مع اللّه احدا»
  4. According to the Quran, certain places can possess special sanctity. «انّ المساجد للّه فلا تدعوا...»
  5. Do not call upon anyone alongside Allah or on par with Him. «فلا تدعوا مع اللّه احدا»
  6. Servitude to Allah is the prerequisite for all perfections and missions. «قام عبداللّه» (Allah bestowed the title ‘Abdullāh (Servant of Allah) upon the Prophet.)
  7. Worshipping Allah requires standing (qiyām), motivation, vitality, and determination. «قام عبداللّه»
  8. In the face of false notions, one must declare one's position explicitly. «قل انّما ادعوا ربّى»
  9. A supplication is valuable only if it is sincere. «ادعوا ربّى ولا اشرك به احدا»
  10. He who calls others to sincerity must himself be sincere. «فلا تدعوا مع اللّه احدا... انّما ادعوا ربّى ولا اشرك به احدا»
  11. Alongside Monotheism (Tawhīd), the negation of polytheism (shirk) is necessary. «ادعوا ربّى ولا اشرك به احدا»
  12. True benefit depends on guidance, and whoever does not accept the path of right direction has incurred loss. Indeed, opposite to right direction, there is nothing but harm. «ضراً و لا رشداً»

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنِّى لَن يُجِيرَنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدٌ وَلَنْ أَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مُلْتَحَدًا (۲۲)
Say, ‘Neither can anyone shelter me from Allah, nor can I find any refuge besides Him. (22)
إِلَّا بَلَـٰغًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِسَـٰلَـٰتِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يَعْصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا (۲۳)
[I have no duty] except to transmit from Allah, and [to communicate] His messages; and whoever disobeys Allah and His apostle, there will indeed be for him the fire of hell, to remain in it forever.’ (23)

Key Points

The word Qul (Say) has appeared in the Quran more than three hundred times, either as an answer to questions that have been raised, or as an answer to questions that might arise, or as a stance against baseless illusions, conjectures, and undue expectations. One of the ways to invite people to sincerity is for a person to consider who can ward off calamities from him: «لن یجیرنى من اللّه احد» (No one can protect me from Allah). The Divine Prophets invited people toward Allah and did not claim any special privilege for themselves. «قل انما انا بشر مثلكم»(60) (Say: I am only a human being like you). «ما ادرى ما یفعل بى»(61) (I do not know what will be done with me). «لا اقول لكم عندى خزائن اللّه»(62) (I do not say to you that I have the treasures of Allah). «لن یجیرنى من اللّه احد» (In the face of Allah, no one can protect me).

60) Kahf, 110. 61) Ahqaf, 9. 62) An'am, 50.

Messages

1- Everyone is equal before the Divine law, and no one has special immunity. «قل انّى لن یجیرنى من اللّه احد»

2- Not only do I not possess power over your profit and loss, but in times of danger, I do not possess the power to ward off calamity from myself either. «لن یجیرنى من اللّه احد»

3- Even the Prophet, if he were to transgress, would not be safe from Divine wrath. «لن یجیرنى من اللّه احد»

4- The only way to be saved from Divine wrath is to fulfill one's duty and mission (Risalah). «الّا بلاغاً... و رسالاته»

5- The Prophet is infallible (Ma'sum), because opposing him has been placed in the same rank as opposing Allah. «و من یعص اللّه و رسوله»

Ayahs

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَأَوْا۟ مَا يُوعَدُونَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَضْعَفُ نَاصِرًا وَأَقَلُّ عَدَدًا (۲٤)
When they see what they are promised, they will know who is weaker in supporters and fewer in numbers. (24)
قُلْ إِنْ أَدْرِىٓ أَقَرِيبٌ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ أَمْ يَجْعَلُ لَهُۥ رَبِّىٓ أَمَدًا (۲٥)
Say, ‘I do not know if what you are promised is near, or if my Lord has set a [long] term for it.’ (25)

Key Points

A group takes pride in saying: «نحن اكثر اموالاً و اولاداً» (Our wealth and children are more numerous). (63) Therefore, the Quran states: In the Resurrection, they will realize that neither money nor wealth and riches will be of use. Not knowing the time of the Resurrection is beneficial, on the one hand, for preparation, and on the other hand, for avoiding anxiety. «قل ان ادرى اقریبٌ»

63) Saba', 35.

Messages

1- The passage of time clarifies matters. «حتى اذا رأوا»

2- Eventually, the heads of the arrogant will hit the rock (they will face harsh reality). «حتى اذا رأوا ما یوعدون»

3- The Day of Resurrection is the day when the weakness and inability of man and all his supports will become manifest. «فسیعلمون من اضعف ناصراً و اقلّ عدداً»

4- Even the Prophet does not know about the future without the Divine Will. «قل ان ادرى»

5- Saying "I do not know" is not a fault. «قل ان ادرى»

Ayahs

عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ فَلَا يُظْهِرُ عَلَىٰ غَيْبِهِۦٓ أَحَدًا (۲٦)
Knower of the Unseen, He does not disclose His [knowledge of the] Unseen to anyone, (26)
إِلَّا مَنِ ٱرْتَضَىٰ مِن رَّسُولٍ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَسْلُكُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦ رَصَدًا (۲۷)
except to an apostle He approves of. Then He dispatches a sentinel before and behind him (27)
لِّيَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ أَبْلَغُوا۟ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ وَأَحْصَىٰ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عَدَدًۢا (۲۸)
so that He may ascertain that they have communicated the messages of their Lord, and He encompasses all that is with them, and He keeps an count of all things. (28)

Key Points

In management, necessary information must be provided to the officials—«عالم الغیب فلا یظهر على غیبه احدا الاّ من ارتضى من رسول»—and He must protect him—«فانه یسلك من بین یدیه...»—and there must be precise supervision over his performance—«لیعلم ان قد ابلغو رسالات ربّهم».

Regarding the knowledge of the Unseen: Does anyone other than Allah know the Unseen or not? If they do, what is the extent of that knowledge and what is its nature? Which individuals know it? Is this knowledge by their own will or not? Is it permanent or momentary? Partial or total? Inherited or gifted? There is much discussion on this. Therefore, I referred to the research I conducted in my youth and the notes I collected years ago, and I present a summary of it here.

Original Principle 1. No one except Allah knows the Unseen independently, because the keys, the treasury, and the source of the knowledge of the Unseen are only with Him: «و عنده مفاتح الغیب لا یعلمها الاّ هو»(64) And if others have a share, it is from Allah. Therefore, the verses that say: We informed someone about the Unseen, mean that We gifted him a portion of the Unseen. «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك»(65) We read in the Quran that Prophet Jesus عليه السلام told the people: I inform you of what you eat and what you store in your houses. «و انبّئكم بما تأكلون و ما تدّخرون فى بیوتكم»(66) Yes, the awareness of others regarding the Unseen is only by the divine will and decree. «و لایحیطون بشى ء من علمه الاّ بما شاء»(67)

Original Principle 2. In Ayah 26 of Surah Al-Jinn, He says: «فلا یظهر على غیبه احداً الاّ من ارتضى من رسول» (meaning, He does not disclose His Unseen to anyone except a Messenger whom He has approved). Meaning, Allah does not inform anyone else about His knowledge of the Unseen, except individuals like the Prophet whom He is pleased with. And in Ayah 179 of Surah Aal-i-Imran, we also read: Allah is not going to inform you of the Unseen, but He chooses from His Messengers whom He wills (and gives him knowledge of the Unseen). «و ما كان اللّه لیطلعكم على الغیب و لكن یجتبى من رسله من یشاء»(68) Therefore, the case of soothsayers, magicians, and fortune-tellers, who have no connection to spirituality and occasionally exploit people's simplicity through their cunning, making predictions based on estimations and analyses, is separate from the case of the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah).

Original Principle 3. The utilization of the knowledge of the Unseen by the friends of Allah is only in specific instances and only by the permission of Allah, not for solving personal problems. Just as the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله acted upon oaths, witnesses, and outward appearances in legal disputes, and did not use the knowledge of the Unseen. Fundamentally, if the friends of Allah were to use miracles and the knowledge of the Unseen for their personal lives and solve their problems, they could not be practical role models for the people. If Imam Husayn عليه السلام had solved the thirst of the desert of Karbala through a miracle and supplication, how could He have been an Imam for people who do not possess such means? One of the secrets to the success of the religious leaders was their patience regarding personal difficulties, wars, poverty, illnesses, bereavement, and the like. And their patience, steadfastness, contentment, forbearance, asceticism (zuhd), and piety were manifested in these situations.

Original Principle 4. The Unseen is of two types: one type that is exclusive to Allah, and another type that is given to the Prophets, the angels, and the infallible Imams. As Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «ان للّه عزّ و جلّ علمین: علماً عنده لم یطّلع علیه احداً من خلقه و علماً نبذه الى ملائكته و رسله فما نبذه الى ملائكته و رسله فقد انتهى الینا»(69) Therefore, the verses that say the knowledge of the Unseen is exclusive to Allah refer to the first type, and the verses that say others know the knowledge of the Unseen refer to the second type. We also read in supplication: O Allah, I swear by the right of that knowledge which You have kept exclusively for Yourself. «وبحقّ علمك الذى استأثرت به لنفسك»

Original Principle 5. The hand of Allah is open in changing the affairs of the world, or, in the Quranic expression, in erasing and establishing matters (mahv wa ithbat), and thus, the friends of Allah cannot have absolute certainty regarding the future. As Imam Sajjad عليه السلام says: If there were not one Ayah in the Quran, I would inform you of all matters until the Day of Resurrection. They asked: Which Ayah? He replied: «یمحوا اللّه ما یشاء و یثبت و عنده امّ الكتاب»(70) Allah erases whatever He wills and establishes whatever He wills, and the Mother of the Book (Umm al-Kitab) is only with Him.(71)

Original Principle 6. Sometimes perfection lies in knowing the Unseen, and sometimes it lies in not knowing it. For example, on the night when Hadrat Ali عليه السلام slept in the Prophet's bed, his perfection lay in not knowing whether the enemies would kill him or not. Because if he had known that they would not kill him and still slept in his place, it would not have been very significant. The religious leaders themselves have said: Sometimes the veil is lifted and we know things, and sometimes it is closed and we cannot know. «یبسط لنا فنعلم و یُقبض عنّا فلا نعلم»(72) Yes, the expansion and contraction (Qabd wa Bast) of the knowledge of the Unseen is in the hand of Allah. Just as He addresses His Prophet in the Quran: «لا تعلمهم»(73) (You do not know the hypocrites of Medina)... and in response to those who ask about the time of the Hour (Resurrection), He says: «قل ان أدرى»(74) Say: I do not know.

64) An‘am, 59. 65) Hud, 49. 66) Aal-i-Imran, 49. 67) Baqarah, 255. 68) Aal-i-Imran, 179. 69) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 255. 70) Ra‘d, 39. 71) Tafsir Al-Mizan, Vol. 11, p. 420. 72) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 256. 73) Tawbah, 101. 74) Jinn, 25.

Messages

  1. Existence is not only what is visible; there are many matters hidden from our eyes and not observable. «عالم الغیب»
  2. Those like fortune-tellers, palm readers, and soothsayers who claim knowledge of the Unseen are liars, because Allah grants the knowledge of the Unseen only to individuals like the Prophets. «الّا من ارتضى من رسول»
  3. No one is independent of an observer and supervisor. «فانّه یسلك... رصداً»
  4. Divine revelation is protected through the angels. «فانّه یسلك من بین یدیه»
  5. The Prophet has immunity (infallibility) in receiving the revelation and conveying it. «رصداً لیعلم ان قد ابلغوا رسالات ربّهم»
  6. Divine protection through the angels is comprehensive. «من بین یدیه و من خلفه رصدا»
  7. The knowledge of Allah is not general, but possesses complete encompassment and enumeration. «لیعلم...احاط... احصى كلّ شى ء عدداً»
